machine.name: Home Chronic Disease Monitor
machine.description: Monitors primary biomarker control, symptom burden, self-management capacity, and lifestyle adherence for adults managing one or more chronic conditions (diabetes, hypertension, COPD, heart failure) at home in low- and medium-income settings. Poor chronic disease control in home care populations is driven by compounding social stressors rather than clinical non-compliance. Output at [2019:2023] feeds the Home Mental Health Access Monitor, reflecting the bidirectional chronic disease — depression comorbidity pathway.

metadata.author: Reality Engine
metadata.populationFocus: Adults with one or more chronic conditions; patients in home-based care programs; low-income adults with limited specialist access

sensorNormalization.biomarker_control_norm: 0.0=critical out-of-range (A1c>10, BP>180/120, O2 sat<88%), 0.5=borderline control (A1c 7.5–9, BP 140–160/90–100), 1.0=target range maintained (A1c<7, BP<130/80)
sensorNormalization.symptom_burden_norm: 0.0=severe daily symptoms limiting function (daily dyspnea, chest pain, severe hypoglycemic episodes), 0.5=moderate but manageable with effort, 1.0=asymptomatic or minimal symptoms
sensorNormalization.self_management_norm: 0.0=unable to self-monitor or self-treat (no glucometer, no BP cuff, no inhaler technique), 0.5=partial self-management (monitoring inconsistently; some technique errors), 1.0=confident and consistent self-management
sensorNormalization.lifestyle_adherence_norm: 0.0=no dietary modification or physical activity; smoking active; high-risk behaviors, 0.5=partial lifestyle change with barriers (diet improved but exercise impossible due to pain/environment), 1.0=fully adherent to prescribed lifestyle plan

governance.notes: Auto-backfilled by scripts/backfill-governance.mjs from primaryDomain="health-personal".  Review and tune the routing as the team owning HomeChronicDiseaseMonitor matures.

rules.description: DISEASE_EXACERBATION: acute disease exacerbation requiring urgent clinical intervention at [2019:2023]
POOR_CONTROL: chronic disease consistently out of target range; complication risk elevated at [2019:2023]
BORDERLINE_CONTROL: chronic disease near but outside target range; preventive intensification warranted at [2019:2023]
DISEASE_CONTROLLED: chronic disease biomarkers in target range; complication risk managed at [2019:2023]

sequences.name: Disease Exacerbation: CONTROLLED+ → SLIPPING → POORLY_CONTROLLED → EXACERBATION → DISEASE_EXACERBATION

metadata.description: Tracks progressive chronic disease control loss leading to acute exacerbation. In home care settings, exacerbation events (diabetic ketoacidosis, hypertensive crisis, COPD exacerbation) are largely preventable with early monitoring. Social stressors — food insecurity, housing instability, caregiver burnout — are the predominant drivers of control loss in this population.

metadata.name: CONTROLLED
metadata.description: CONTROLLED+ — primary biomarkers in or near target range; symptoms manageable; self-monitoring occurring; lifestyle partially adherent. isInitial A*A = A+.

metadata.name: SLIPPING
metadata.description: SLIPPING — biomarkers trending toward out-of-range; symptom burden increasing; self-management inconsistency.

metadata.name: POORLY_CONTROLLED
metadata.description: POORLY_CONTROLLED — biomarkers significantly out of range; daily symptoms; self-management breaking down.

metadata.name: EXACERBATION
metadata.description: EXACERBATION — critical biomarker values; severe symptoms; self-management collapsed. Fires DISEASE_EXACERBATION requiring urgent intervention.
DISEASE_EXACERBATION: acute disease exacerbation requiring urgent clinical intervention at [2019:2023]
metadata.action: Urgent nurse visit; physician notification; ER transport if critical; medication review; social barrier assessment

metadata.description: Fires POOR_CONTROL when chronic disease biomarkers are consistently out of target range without acute exacerbation — the sustained inadequate control state that drives long-term complications (retinopathy, nephropathy, peripheral vascular disease) in low-income diabetic and hypertensive populations.

metadata.name: STRUGGLING
metadata.description: STRUGGLING+ — biomarkers consistently poor but not acute; symptom burden moderate; self-management attempted but inadequate. isInitial A*A = A+: fires POOR_CONTROL each matching cycle.
metadata.scenario: Diabetic patient with A1c consistently 9.5–10.5; no acute event but organ damage accumulating

metadata.description: POOR_CONTROL: chronic disease consistently out of target range; complication risk elevated at [2019:2023]
metadata.action: Intensified home monitoring; medication review; nutrition counseling; CHW support for social barriers

metadata.description: Fires BORDERLINE_CONTROL when disease metrics are in the near-miss zone — adequate enough to avoid acute events but insufficient for complication prevention. Common in working-poor patients managing diet and lifestyle without full access to chronic disease support programs.

metadata.name: BORDERLINE
metadata.description: BORDERLINE+ — biomarkers near but outside target; symptom burden low; self-management inconsistent but attempting. isInitial A*A = A+: fires BORDERLINE_CONTROL each matching cycle.
metadata.scenario: Hypertensive patient running 140s/90s; not at target but managing; diet compliance partial

metadata.description: BORDERLINE_CONTROL: chronic disease near but outside target range; preventive intensification warranted at [2019:2023]
metadata.action: Medication titration review; DSMES referral; dietary counseling; home visit frequency increase

metadata.description: Fires DISEASE_CONTROLLED when biomarkers are in target range and self-management is functioning. In low-income home care, this outcome reflects successful care coordination and social barrier resolution — not just clinical compliance.

metadata.name: WELL_CONTROLLED
metadata.description: WELL_CONTROLLED+ — biomarkers at or near target; symptoms controlled; self-management consistent; lifestyle partially adherent. Fires DISEASE_CONTROLLED each matching cycle.
metadata.scenario: Diabetic patient on Medicaid with CHW support; A1c <7.5; consistent self-monitoring

metadata.description: DISEASE_CONTROLLED: chronic disease biomarkers in target range; complication risk managed at [2019:2023]
metadata.action: Mental health access monitor registers chronic disease stable

inputSequences.name: Diabetic Control Loss During Job Loss
inputSequences.description: Patient loses job and health insurance; stops affording insulin; blood sugar spirals to DKA; ER visit; Medicaid emergency enrollment; returns to home management.
